subject: [PATCH v2 0/7] mm: switch THP shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about the lockdep splat and suggested using explicit rcu_read_lock() annotation to fix it. Also requested splitting out list_lru prep bits for easier review.

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch series aims to switch the THP (Transparent Huge Pages) shrinker from its current implementation to use list_lru instead. The change involves refactoring and cleaning up existing code, extending the list_lru API to accommodate private state synchronization, and finally switching the deferred_split_queue to list_lru.

reviews.author: syzbot ci
reviews.summary: The reviewer, syzbot ci, reported a WARNING in lock_list_lru_of_memcg and a possible deadlock in __folio_end_writeback, indicating potential issues with the list_lru implementation.
reviews.raw_body:
syzbot ci has tested the following series

[v2] mm: switch THP shrinker to list_lru
https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260312205321.638053-1-hannes@cmpxchg.org
* [PATCH v2 1/7] mm: list_lru: lock_list_lru_of_memcg() cannot return NULL if !skip_empty
* [PATCH v2 2/7] mm: list_lru: deduplicate unlock_list_lru()
* [PATCH v2 3/7] mm: list_lru: move list dead check to lock_list_lru_of_memcg()
* [PATCH v2 4/7] mm: list_lru: deduplicate lock_list_lru()
* [PATCH v2 5/7] mm: list_lru: introduce caller locking for additions and deletions
* [PATCH v2 6/7] mm: list_lru: introduce memcg_list_lru_alloc_folio()
* [PATCH v2 7/7] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

and found the following issues:
* WARNING in lock_list_lru_of_memcg
* possible deadlock in __folio_end_writeback

Full report is available here:
https://ci.syzbot.org/series/e7f4d9e2-b111-4e6e-80f8-e762d8337560

***

WARNING in lock_list_lru_of_memcg

possible deadlock in __folio_end_writeback

tree:      mm-new
URL:       https://kernel.googlesource.com/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/akpm/mm.git
base:      f543926f9d0c3f6dfb354adfe7fbaeedd1277c6b
arch:      amd64

syz repro: https://ci.syzbot.org/findings/8c08a79f-a08c-41d5-95e6-2860caf8744c/syz_repro

=====================================================
WARNING: SOFTIRQ-safe -> SOFTIRQ-unsafe lock order detected
syzkaller #0 Not tainted
-----------------------------------------------------
syz.0.17/5949 [HC0[0]:SC0[0]:HE0:SE1] is trying to acquire:
ffff88810c90c240 (&l->lock){+.+.}-{3:3}, at: spin_lock include/linux/spinlock.h:341 [inline]
ffff88810c90c240 (&l->lock){+.+.}-{3:3}, at: lock_list_lru mm/list_lru.c:26 [inline]
ffff88810c90c240 (&l->lock){+.+.}-{3:3}, at: lock_list_lru_of_memcg+0x268/0x470 mm/list_lru.c:95

and this task is already holding:
ffff8881107ad160 (&xa->xa_lock#9){..-.}-{3:3}, at: spin_lock include/linux/spinlock.h:341 [inline]
ffff8881107ad160 (&xa->xa_lock#9){..-.}-{3:3}, at: __folio_split+0xa2e/0x1570 mm/huge_memory.c:4025
which would create a new lock dependency:
 (&xa->xa_lock#9){..-.}-{3:3} -> (&l->lock){+.+.}-{3:3}

but this new dependency connects a SOFTIRQ-irq-safe lock:
 (&xa->xa_lock#9){..-.}-{3:3}

... which became SOFTIRQ-irq-safe at:

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a technical issue with the deferred_split_lru, specifically that list_lru_lock() is being called on a nid+memcg combination without allocated list_lru heads, and agreed that more thought is needed to resolve this.
sentiment_signals: agreed that more thought is needed
reviews.raw_body:
File pages aren't on the deferred_split_lru. We're calling
list_lru_lock() on a nid+memcg combination that doesn't have list_lru
heads allocated. This should either fail gracefully or needs page type
filtering in __folio_freeze_and_split_unmapped(). Needs more thought.

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch series switches the THP (Transparent Huge Pages) shrinker to use the list_lru data structure instead of its current implementation. The motivation for this change is to address scalability issues in the original implementation, which involved a complex sequence of function calls. The new approach uses the list_lru API to manage the LRU state and allows for more fine-grained control over locking. The patches also include various cleanups and refactors to make the code easier to review and maintain.

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that reparenting is a delicate dance and suggested adding a comment to explain this, but since it's already covered in the code, no further action is required.
reviews.raw_body:
Was about to suggest adding a comment regarding reparenting, but the
comment further below already spells that out now.

Reviewed-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer David (Arm) noted that the two 'bool' parameters in lock_list_lru_of_memcg() are ugly and suggested adding /*skip=empty=*/true to make the callers easier to read, but considered this a minor issue for later cleanup.

Reviewer David pointed out a typo in the patch, specifically that the string 'false' was used instead of 'NULL', which is likely causing confusion and requested it be corrected.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
The two "bool" parameters really are ugly. Fortunately this is only an
internal function.

The callers are still a bit hard to read; we could add /*skip=empty=*/true).

like

return lock_list_lru_of_memcg(lru, nid, memcg, /* irq= */false, NULL,
			      /* skip_empty= */false);

Like we do in other code. But I guess we should do it consistently then
(or better add some proper flags).

Anyhow, something that could be cleaned up later.

---

And here it gets really confusing. true false false ... am I reading
binary code?

I guess the second "false" should actually be "NULL" :)

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the function name should be more descriptive, suggesting either folio_memcg_list_lru_alloc() or memcg_list_lru_alloc_for_folio() to clearly indicate allocation for a folio.
reviews.raw_body:
The function reads as if we would be allocating a folio ...

folio_memcg_list_lru_alloc() ?

Or memcg_list_lru_alloc_for_folio() ?


LGTM, with my limited understanding of memcg lifetimes :)

Reviewed-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agrees to restructure in v2.

Author agrees that the THP shrinker conversion needs a v3 patch to fix issues in __folio_freeze_and_split_unmapped() and lockdep key, and plans to address these in the next version.
sentiment_signals: plans to address issues in v3
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, I absolutely hate this too. I only didn't look further because
it's internal, but...

---

This is a great idea.

I have to send a v3 for the fix in __folio_freeze_and_split_unmapped()
and the lockdep key, so I'll make this change along with it.

2026-03-17.patch_summary: This patch series switches the THP (Transparent Huge Pages) shrinker to use the list_lru data structure instead of its current implementation. The motivation for this change is to address scalability issues in the original implementation, which involved a complex sequence of function calls. The new approach uses the list_lru API to manage the LRU state and allows for more fine-grained control over locking. The patches also include various cleanups and refactors to make the code easier to review and maintain.
